channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: ☢️ Belarus Launches Joint Nuclear Drills with Russia
item.description: Belarusian and Russian forces began joint training exercises on nuclear weapons combat, drawing strong criticism from Ukraine and NATO allies who warn the Kremlin is turning Belarus into a nuclear staging ground near NATO borders. | Source: Al Jazeera | Category: Europe

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Launches Largest Drone Attack on Moscow in Over a Year
item.description: Ukraine struck Russia with nearly 600 drones across 14 regions overnight, killing at least four people including three in the Moscow area, in what officials call Kyiv's biggest drone strike on the Russian capital in more than a year. | Source: The Guardian | Category: Ukraine-Russia War

item.title: 🇫🇷 France Pitches New Africa Partnership Model at Kenya Summit
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron opened the Africa Forward Summit in Nairobi, France's first major summit in an English-speaking African country, promoting a "partnership of equals" as Paris pivots away from its troubled relationship with former colonies. | Source: AP News | Category: Africa

item.title: 🇱🇻 Latvia Prime Minister Resigns Over Ukrainian Drone Controversy
item.description: Latvia's Prime Minister Evika Silina resigned after a coalition partner withdrew support over her handling of stray Ukrainian drones that crashed into Latvian territory, exposing political tensions in a NATO border state. | Source: France 24 | Category: Europe

item.title: 🇮🇱 Hamas Military Wing Leader Killed in Israeli Airstrike
item.description: Israel confirmed killing Izz al-Din al-Haddad, one of the last surviving architects of the October 7 attacks, in a Gaza airstrike. Hamas confirmed the death amid an ongoing fragile ceasefire. | Source: AP News | Category: Middle East

item.title: 🇷🇺 First Russian Student Recruited From University Dies in Ukraine
item.description: Valery Averin, 23, became the first known Russian recruited directly from a university to die in Ukraine, exposing Moscow's campaign to recruit students into military service through financial incentives. | Source: Kyiv Independent | Category: Ukraine-Russia War

item.title: 🇦🇺 Australia Orders Chinese Investors to Divest From Rare Earths Company
item.description: The Australian government ordered Chinese investors to divest from Northern Minerals, a rare earths company. Analysts say the move reflects efforts to diversify supply chains away from China but risks Beijing retaliation. | Source: SCMP | Category: Geopolitics

item.title: 🇮🇪 Irish President's Sister Among Gaza Flotilla Activists Detained by Israel
item.description: Israeli forces intercepted a Gaza-bound aid flotilla and detained eight Irish citizens, including the sister of Ireland's president. Dublin condemned the interception as "illegal kidnapping" of humanitarian activists. | Source: Daily Sabah | Category: Middle East

item.title: 🇧🇴 Bolivia faces largest protests in decades as Evo Morales supporters join movement
item.description: Followers of former President Evo Morales have joined massive protests against President Rodrigo Paz, marking the biggest challenge to Bolivia's first conservative leader after nearly two decades of socialist rule. The protests are fueled by the nation's worst economic crisis in a generation. | Source: AP News | Category: Latin America

item.title: 🇬🇱 US demands major role in Greenland through closed-door negotiations
item.description: Confidential talks between the US, Greenland, and Denmark reveal American demands for indefinite military presence and veto power over major investment deals in Greenland, aimed at countering Russian and Chinese influence in the Arctic. Greenlandic officials fear the demands would compromise their sovereignty for generations. | Source: New York Times | Category: Arctic

item.title: 🇦🇪 Drone strike targets UAE nuclear power plant amid Iran conflict tensions
item.description: A drone strike hit the Barakah nuclear power plant in the UAE, setting an electrical generator on fire. No one claimed responsibility, but suspicion fell on Iran as regional tensions remain high. Nuclear safety experts warn that nuclear facilities must not be targets in conflicts. | Source: CBS News | Category: Middle East

item.title: 🇮🇷 US-Iran Standoff Deepens as Neither Side Bends
item.description: Three months after the US-Israel attack on Iran, a US blockade and Tehran's grip on the Strait of Hormuz have created a deadlock with neither side willing to compromise, raising the risk of renewed war. | Source: Reuters | Category: US-Iran War

item.title: 💰 Opaque Oil Deals Around Hormuz Test the Petrodollar
item.description: The US dollar-dominated global oil trading system is being tested by the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz closure, as governments turn to increasingly opaque deals with Tehran and Gulf producers to secure supplies. | Source: Reuters | Category: Economy

item.title: 🛢️ India's Cooking Fuel Shortage Driving Up California Gas Prices
item.description: A cooking gas shortage in India and $6 per gallon gasoline in California are both symptoms of the worst-ever energy supply disruption caused by the Iran war, demonstrating the global interconnectedness of fuel markets. | Source: Reuters | Category: Energy

item.title: 🇮🇩 Indonesian Rupiah Hits Record Low Despite Central Bank Intervention
item.description: The Indonesian rupiah slid to another record low despite central bank interventions, as global oil prices surged to two-week highs, though President Prabowo downplayed the impact on citizens. | Source: Reuters | Category: Asia-Pacific

item.title: 🇨🇳🇹🇼 China Rejects Taiwan Sovereignty Claims After Leader Pledges Never to Be Sacrificed
item.description: Beijing stated Taiwan was "never a country" after Taiwanese leader William Lai Ching-te said the island would never be traded away, in the latest exchange over cross-strait sovereignty. | Source: Anadolu Agency | Category: Asia-Pacific

item.title: 🇩🇪🇹🇷🇪🇺 Germany Calls for Stronger EU-Turkey Ties, Says Ankara Key to NATO Defense
item.description: German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul said Turkey should be included as the EU develops its defense policy, citing shared security interests and Turkiye role as a critical NATO ally. | Source: Anadolu Agency | Category: Europe

item.title: 🇮🇱🏛️ Israel Opposition Demands Knesset Dissolution, Early Elections Amid Coalition Strains
item.description: Israeli opposition leaders urged lawmakers to support a bill for preliminary vote next week to dissolve parliament, as the governing coalition faces mounting internal political pressure. | Source: Anadolu Agency | Category: Middle East

item.title: ⚠️ Belarus and Russia Conduct Joint Nuclear Weapons Drills
item.description: Belarus launched military exercises practicing the use of Russian tactical nuclear weapons deployed on its territory, drawing strong condemnation from Ukraine. | Source: Reuters | Category: Europe

item.title: ⚔️☢️ SCMP Covers Belarus Nuclear Exercises Near NATO Borders
item.description: Belarus began training exercises involving Russian tactical nuclear weapons, testing military readiness to deploy nuclear arms across different regions of the country bordering three NATO states. | Source: SCMP | Category: Europe

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan Deploys Fighter Jets and Troops to Saudi Arabia Amid Iran War
item.description: Pakistan is ramping up military cooperation with Riyadh, deploying a jet squadron and thousands of troops, even as Islamabad serves as the main mediator in the Iran-US conflict. | Source: Reuters | Category: Middle East

item.title: 🇮🇷 Pakistan Conveys New Iranian Peace Proposal to United States
item.description: Iran sent a revised peace proposal to the US via Pakistani mediators with terms similar to previously rejected offers, though a senior Iranian official claims Washington has softened on some issues. | Source: Reuters | Category: Diplomacy

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 China Commits to $17 Billion in Annual US Agricultural Purchases
item.description: Following the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing, China agreed to buy at least $17 billion annually in US farm products for three years, marking a significant trade commitment amid broader diplomatic engagement. | Source: Reuters | Category: US-China

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia Strikes Odesa and Dnipro in Overnight Attacks
item.description: Russian forces targeted Ukrainian cities of Odesa and Dnipro with missiles and drones, damaging residential buildings and injuring dozens, while Ukrainian drone strikes killed two in Russia. | Source: Reuters | Category: Ukraine-Russia War

item.title: 🇷🇺🇨🇳 Kremlin Has High Expectations for Putin's China Visit
item.description: Russia expressed serious expectations for President Putin's upcoming trip to China this week, where he will meet Xi Jinping to discuss developing their "privileged partnership" amid the Iran war and Ukraine conflict. | Source: Reuters | Category: Diplomacy
